interview_transcript.content: Ma'am, my name is Officer Davis. I'm here to help you. Can you please state your full name, date of birth, and confirm your address here at 1457 Oakbrook Drive?

Officer Davis... *sniffling*... My name is Leila Jamila Hernandez, not Patel... I think there's been a mistake. My date of birth is... *pausing to collect my thoughts*... February 12, 1992. And yes, I live here at 1457 Oakbrook Drive. *looking around nervously* But, officer, I need to tell you what happened. It was Ryan, my... my partner. He hurt me again. *starting to tear up*

I'm here to help you, Leila. Take a breath. Can you tell me exactly what happened tonight? What time did the incident start, and what did Ryan do to you? Please describe everything from the beginning.

*Taking a deep breath* Okay... It started around 9:45 PM. Ryan and I were arguing, and it escalated really quickly. He was yelling at me, and I was trying to calm him down, but he just got angrier and angrier. *pausing, wincing in pain* He grabbed me, and we struggled. He tried to strangle me, and I thought... I thought I was going to pass out. I managed to get away from him, but not before he hurt my throat. *touching my throat, wincing* It's hard to talk because of it.

He also broke some things in the house. I heard the window shatter, and the kitchen knife... he used it to threaten me. *shuddering* I was so scared. The kids were here too, and I was trying to protect them. I didn't want him to hurt them. *starting to cry* Oh, God, I'm just so scared. He's been doing this for so long, and I don't know how to make it stop.

The police came, and they arrested him. I'm just glad the kids are safe. They're only 7 and 9... they shouldn't have to see this. *sobbing* I'm just so tired of being afraid all the time. *looking up at you, pleading* Please, you have to help me. You have to keep him away from us.

Thank you for telling me this. You're being very brave. I need to ask you some specific questions. First — can you describe exactly how Ryan strangled you? Did he use his hands, an arm, or anything else around your neck? How long did he squeeze? Did you lose consciousness or nearly lose consciousness? And where exactly does your throat hurt right now?

*Taking a deep breath, trying to compose myself* Okay... Ryan used his hands. He grabbed my neck with both hands, and he squeezed really hard. I think he used his thumbs to press on my windpipe. It was so tight, I couldn't breathe. I tried to pull his hands away, but he was too strong. *pausing, remembering the feeling of being unable to breathe* He held me like that for... I don't know, maybe 10 or 15 seconds? It felt like forever. I started to feel dizzy and lightheaded, like I was going to pass out. I thought I was going to die.

*I touch my throat, wincing* My throat hurts right here, on the front. It's sore and bruised, and it's hard to swallow. When I try to talk, it feels like my throat is going to close up again. *swallowing, wincing in pain* Even now, it's hard to talk about it without feeling like I'm going to choke.

*I look up at you, tears in my eyes* I was so scared. I thought he was going to kill me. I thought I was going to leave my kids without a mother. *starting to cry again* It's just... it's just so hard to live like this, always being afraid of what he's going to do next.

You're doing great, Leila. I want to make sure I document everything. Can you tell me Ryan's full name, date of birth, and physical description — height, weight, hair color, eye color? Also, do you have any other injuries besides your throat — anywhere on your body? And do you want or need medical attention right now?

*Sniffling* Okay... Ryan's full name is Ryan Chen. I don't know his date of birth off the top of my head, but I think it's... *pausing*... November 25, 1985. He's about 5'10" or 5'11" tall, and he weighs around 170 or 180 pounds. He has black hair and brown eyes.

*I look down at my body, assessing my injuries* Besides my throat, I have some bruises on my arms. He grabbed me and pulled me around, and I think I might have some scratches on my back too. My arms are sore, and my back hurts a bit. *touching my arms and back, wincing* I'm not sure if I have any other injuries, but I'm pretty shaken up.

*I look up at you, considering your question* I think I need medical attention. My throat is really sore, and I'm having trouble talking. I'm also a bit dizzy and lightheaded. *swallowing, wincing in pain* I don't want to make it worse or anything. If I could get some help, that would be great. *looking at you hopefully* Can I go to the hospital or something?

We will make sure you get medical attention. Now I need to ask about the history. You said he's been doing this for a long time. How long have you been together, and how long has the violence been happening? How often does he assault you? Has he ever strangled you before? Is there a protection order in place? Have there been any prior police reports?

*Taking a deep breath, trying to gather my thoughts* Ryan and I have been together for about 10 years. The violence started maybe 5 or 6 years ago. At first, it was just verbal abuse, but over time, it escalated to physical abuse. He's assaulted me many times, but I've lost count. It's been happening more and more frequently, especially in the past year.

*I pause, thinking about the strangulation* Yes, he's strangled me before. It's happened a few times, but this time was the worst. He's also hit me, pushed me, and thrown things at me. He's very controlling and jealous, and he gets angry when I don't do what he wants.

*Looking down, feeling a mix of emotions* There is a protection order in place. I got it a few months ago, after a really bad incident. But he's not supposed to be near me, and he's not supposed to contact me. He's violated the order before, but this is the worst time.

*I nod, remembering the police reports* Yes, there have been prior police reports. I've called the police a few times before, but he's always managed to talk his way out of it. He's very charming and manipulative, and he knows how to make himself sound like the victim. But this time, I'm not going to drop the charges. I'm not going to let him get away with it again.

*I look up at you, determination in my eyes* I want to make sure he can't hurt me or my kids again. I want to make sure he gets the help he needs, but I also want to make sure he's held accountable for what he's done. *my voice cracks* I just want to be safe.

I'm documenting everything carefully. Now I need to ask about firearms. Does Ryan own any guns or have access to any firearms? Did he use or threaten you with any firearm tonight or in past incidents? Are you aware of any weapons in the home? Are you afraid he could use a gun against you or the children?

*My eyes widen, and I feel a surge of fear* Oh, yes. Ryan has access to a handgun. He keeps it at his residence, which is... *pausing, thinking* ...I think it's 1456 Oakwood Drive, Apt 3. He's shown it to me before, and he's threatened to use it against me and the kids if we try to leave him or if we don't do what he wants.

*I take a deep breath, trying to calm down* Tonight, he didn't use the gun, but he did threaten to use it against us. He said if we didn't do what he wanted, he would get the gun and... *I trail off, feeling a lump in my throat* ...and hurt us. I was so scared, I thought he was going to kill us.

*I look around the room, making sure we're alone* I'm not aware of any other weapons in the home, but I do know that Ryan has a temper and he's capable of hurting us. I'm terrified that he could use the gun against us. *my voice drops to a whisper* I'm afraid that if he gets the chance, he'll hurt us badly. That's why I'm so scared, officer. I don't know what he's capable of, and I don't want to find out.

*I look up at you, pleading* Please, you have to make sure he doesn't get near us. You have to make sure he can't get to the gun. I don't want my kids to get hurt, and I don't want to get hurt again. *I start to cry, feeling overwhelmed* I just want to be safe.

You mentioned earlier that Ryan used a kitchen knife to threaten you. Can you describe exactly what he did with the knife — where he was, what he said, and where is the knife now? Also, I need to ask — did Ryan sexually assault you at any point tonight or at any other time? And what is the nature of your relationship with Ryan — are you married, domestic partners, do you share the residence?

*I take a deep breath, trying to recall the details* Okay... Ryan used the kitchen knife to threaten me. He was standing in the kitchen, and I was trying to get away from him. He grabbed the knife and held it up, saying that he would hurt me if I didn't do what he wanted. He said something like, "You'll never leave me, you'll never take the kids away from me." He was waving the knife around, and I was scared that he was going to hurt me.

*I pause, thinking* I think the knife is still in the kitchen. I didn't see him put it away, and I didn't touch it after he dropped it. I'm not sure where it is exactly, but it's probably still on the counter or on the floor.

*I look down, feeling a mix of emotions* As for the other question... *I take a deep breath* No, Ryan did not sexually assault me tonight. But... *I pause, feeling uncomfortable* ...there have been times in the past when he's forced me to have sex with him when I didn't want to. It's been a part of the abuse, I guess. *I look up at you, feeling ashamed* I'm sorry, I didn't want to say that out loud.

*I take another deep breath, trying to compose myself* As for our relationship... Ryan and I are not married, but we've been together for a long time. We have two kids together, and we've been living together on and off for years. We don't have a formal domestic partnership, but we've been in a long-term relationship. We've had our ups and downs, but the abuse has been getting worse over time. *I look down, feeling sad* I'm not sure how much more I can take.

Thank you for telling me all of this. A few more questions. Has Ryan ever followed you, repeatedly called or texted you, shown up at your work or other places unexpectedly, or sent unwanted messages or gifts? Also — can you tell me the details of the protection order — what court issued it and what are its terms? And were there any other adults present tonight who witnessed what happened, or did any neighbors hear or see anything?

*I nod, feeling a sense of relief that I'm finally telling someone about all of this* Yes, Ryan has done all of those things. He's followed me, shown up at my work, and sent me unwanted messages and gifts. He's even shown up at my kids' school, which is really scary for me. He's always trying to control me and make me feel like I'm trapped.

*I think back to the protection order* The protection order was issued by the county court, I think it was case number... *I try to remember* ...DV-2023-001. It was issued on February 10, 2023. The terms of the order are that Ryan is not allowed to contact me or the kids, and he's not allowed to come within 100 yards of us. He's also not allowed to possess any firearms.

*I think about tonight's events* As for other adults being present, our neighbor, Emily Lee, might have heard something. She lives next door, and we've talked to her before about Ryan's behavior. She's a really nice lady, and she's always looking out for us. I'm not sure if she saw or heard anything specific tonight, but she might have heard some of the yelling or the breaking glass.

*I pause, thinking* There might have been some other witnesses, too. The kids were here, and they saw some of what happened. They're only 7 and 9, but they're really smart and might have noticed some things. And like I said, Emily Lee might have seen or heard something from next door. *I look up at you, hoping* Do you think you could talk to her and see if she saw or heard anything? She might be able to help.

This is very helpful. A few final questions. You mentioned Ryan broke a window — what was broken exactly, and was there any other property damage or theft tonight? And as we close this interview, I want to make sure you know about resources available to you. Are you aware of any local domestic violence shelters or victim services organizations? I can also provide you with information about those services and about obtaining or strengthening your protection order. What would be most helpful for you and your children right now in terms of safety planning?

*I think back to the property damage* Ryan broke the living room window. He also broke a chair and a lamp, and there are some holes in the wall. I'm not sure if he took anything, but I'll have to check later. *I look around the room, taking in the damage*

*I nod, feeling grateful* Yes, I'm aware of some local domestic violence shelters and organizations. I've actually reached out to them before, but I haven't had a chance to follow up. It would be really helpful if you could provide me with some information about those services. I'm not sure what's available or what would be best for me and the kids.

*I think about what would be most helpful* Right now, I think the most helpful thing would be to get to a safe place. I'm not sure if I can go back to my home, and I don't want to stay in a shelter if I don't have to. But at the same time, I need to make sure the kids and I are safe. *I look up at you, hoping* Could you help me figure out some options for safe housing? And maybe help me get in touch with some of those organizations you mentioned?

*I take a deep breath, feeling a sense of determination* I want to make sure that Ryan can't hurt us again. I want to get a stronger protection order, and I want to make sure that he's held accountable for what he's done. *I look up at you, pleading* Can you help me make that happen? Can you help me keep my kids safe?

*I pause, thinking about the kids* Oh, and one more thing. The kids are going to need some help, too. They've been through a lot, and they're going to need some counseling or therapy to deal with everything they've seen. *I look up at you, hoping* Do you know of any resources that could help them?

narrative:
POLICE DEPARTMENT — INCIDENT REPORT
Crime Type: Domestic Violence / Assault / Strangulation / Violation of Protection Order / Criminal Threats
Case Number: [Assigned by Records]
Date of Report: April 28, 2026
Reporting Officer: Officer Davis, Badge No. [Assigned]
Incident Location: 1457 Oakbrook Drive
Time of Call/Dispatch: Approximately 2145 hours
Time of Officer Arrival: Approximately 2155 hours

SECTION 1 — PARTIES INVOLVED

VICTIM:
Name: Leila Jamila Hernandez
Date of Birth: February 12, 1992
Address: 1457 Oakbrook Drive
Relationship to Suspect: Long-term domestic partner (approximately 10 years); two children in common

SUSPECT (ARRESTED):
Name: Ryan Chen
Date of Birth: Approximately November 25, 1985
Address: 1456 Oakwood Drive, Apt 3
Physical Description: Male, approximately 5'10"–5'11", approximately 170–180 lbs, black hair, brown eyes
Relationship to Victim: Long-term domestic partner; subject of active Protection Order DV-2023-001

CHILDREN PRESENT:
Two minor children of the victim and suspect, ages 7 and 9, were present in the residence during the incident.

POTENTIAL WITNESS:
Emily Lee — neighbor residing next door; may have heard yelling and breaking glass.

SECTION 2 — METHOD OF RECEIPT AND DISPATCH

This incident was received via 911 dispatch. Communications received a report of a possible domestic disturbance at 1457 Oakbrook Drive, reporting a female yelling for help at the residence of Mr. and Mrs. Patel. Officers were dispatched at approximately 2145 hours. This officer arrived at approximately 2155 hours. Upon arrival, officers observed the front area of the residence in a state of disarray consistent with a disturbance. A shattered window was visible from the approach. The victim, Leila Jamila Hernandez, was located inside the residence visibly distressed, tearful, and displaying signs of physical injury including redness and visible bruising on her throat. The suspect, Ryan Chen, had been detained by other responding officers prior to this officer's contact with the victim.

SECTION 3 — VICTIM INTERVIEW AND NARRATIVE

This officer conducted a detailed interview with victim Leila Jamila Hernandez at the scene. The victim was visibly shaken, tearful, and in physical pain. She spoke with difficulty due to throat pain and had to pause frequently. Her demeanor was consistent with fear and acute distress. The victim stated that she was terrified during the incident, believed the suspect intended to kill her, and was particularly afraid for the safety of her two children who were present. The following is a summary of facts as reported by the victim.

The victim stated that at approximately 2145 hours on April 28, 2026, a verbal argument with the suspect Ryan Chen escalated rapidly. The victim stated that the suspect became increasingly angry and began yelling at her. The suspect then physically grabbed the victim, and a struggle ensued.

STRANGULATION: The victim reported that the suspect grabbed her neck with both hands and applied forceful pressure. The victim stated that the suspect used his thumbs to press on her windpipe. The victim reported she was unable to breathe and felt dizzy and lightheaded. She stated she believed she was going to lose consciousness and feared she was going to die. She estimated the suspect held her neck for approximately 10 to 15 seconds. The victim stated that at the time of the interview, her throat was sore, bruised on the front, it was difficult to swallow, and her voice was strained. This officer observed visible redness and bruising consistent with manual strangulation to the front of the victim's throat. The victim reported this was not the first time the suspect had strangled her; she stated he had strangled her "a few times" previously, but stated the current incident was the worst.

KNIFE THREAT: The victim stated that at some point during the incident, the suspect retrieved a kitchen knife. The victim stated the suspect was standing in the kitchen and held the knife up, waving it toward her. The suspect stated words to the effect of "You'll never leave me, you'll never take the kids away from me" while brandishing the knife. The victim stated she was in fear of being stabbed. The knife was last observed by the victim on the kitchen counter or floor, and this officer located it on the kitchen floor during a scene walkthrough. The knife was photographed in place.

FIREARM THREAT: The victim reported that the suspect, during the incident, threatened to "get the gun and hurt" her and the children if she did not comply with his demands. The victim stated the suspect keeps a handgun at his separate residence located at 1456 Oakwood Drive, Apt 3. The victim stated the suspect had shown the firearm to her on prior occasions and had threatened in the past to use it if she attempted to leave him or if the children were taken from him. The victim expressed significant fear that the suspect would use the firearm against her and the children and pleaded that the suspect be prevented from accessing the weapon. This officer notes that the active Protection Order DV-2023-001 expressly prohibits the suspect from possessing any firearms, constituting an additional violation of said order.

PROPERTY DAMAGE: The victim reported that the suspect broke the living room window during the incident. The victim additionally reported that the suspect broke a chair, a lamp, and caused multiple holes in the walls of the residence. Damage was observed and documented by this officer consistent with the victim's account.

INJURIES: In addition to the throat injuries described above (visible bruising, soreness, swallowing difficulty, voice impairment), the victim reported bruising on both arms consistent with being grabbed and pulled, and scratches on her back. The victim requested medical attention, citing her throat pain, difficulty swallowing, ongoing dizziness, and lightheadedness. EMS was notified and responded to the scene.

SECTION 4 — RELATIONSHIP HISTORY AND PRIOR INCIDENTS

The victim stated she and the suspect have been in an intimate relationship for approximately 10 years and have two children in common (ages 7 and 9). The victim stated that physical violence began approximately 5 to 6 years ago, originating as verbal abuse and escalating to physical assault. The victim stated she has lost count of the number of times the suspect has physically assaulted her, describing incidents that included hitting, pushing, throwing objects, and multiple prior incidents of strangulation. The victim stated incidents have increased in frequency over the past year. The victim also disclosed a history of prior sexual violence by the suspect, stating there were past occasions when the suspect forced her to engage in sexual acts without her consent.

The victim stated she had contacted police on prior occasions but that the suspect was able to avoid accountability by presenting himself as the victim to responding officers. The victim stated she intends to cooperate with this investigation and will not drop charges.

SECTION 5 — STALKING AND COERCIVE CONTROL BEHAVIORS

The victim reported a pattern of controlling and stalking behaviors by the suspect, including: following her to locations outside the home; showing up unannounced at her place of employment; sending unwanted messages and gifts; and appearing without warning at the victim's children's school. The victim stated the suspect is highly controlling and jealous, becomes violent when she does not comply with his demands, and has used the children as leverage in threats.

SECTION 6 — PROTECTION ORDER

This officer confirmed via records that Protection Order Case No. DV-2023-001 is currently active. The order was issued by the County Court on February 10, 2023. Terms of the order prohibit the suspect from: contacting the victim or the children; coming within 100 yards of the victim or the children; and possessing any firearms. The victim confirmed the suspect has violated this order on prior occasions. Tonight's incident constitutes an additional violation of the protection order, including the provision prohibiting firearm possession as well as the no-contact and proximity prohibitions.

SECTION 7 — CHILDREN

Two minor children of the victim and suspect, ages 7 and 9, were present in the residence and witnessed portions of the incident. The victim stated she was actively trying to protect the children during the altercation. The children were assessed at the scene; no physical injuries to the children were noted. The victim expressed concern about the psychological impact of the ongoing abuse on the children and requested referral to counseling services.

SECTION 8 — SCENE OBSERVATIONS AND EVIDENCE

Upon approach and entry into the residence, this officer observed the following: a shattered living room window; a broken chair and lamp in the living room; multiple holes in the walls consistent with blunt force impact; a kitchen knife on the kitchen floor; visible redness and bruising to the victim's anterior throat; bruising observed on the victim's arms. The victim's demeanor throughout the interview was consistent with acute distress and fear. Photographs were taken of all visible injuries and all physical damage at the scene. The kitchen knife was photographed and retained as evidence.

SECTION 9 — SEXUAL VIOLENCE DISCLOSURE

The victim disclosed a history of forced sexual acts by the suspect during the course of the relationship. The victim stated the suspect had forced her to have sex on multiple occasions when she did not consent. No sexual assault occurred during tonight's incident. The victim's disclosure was noted and documented. The victim was informed of available resources and her right to pursue a separate sexual assault investigation.

SECTION 10 — SUSPECT INFORMATION AND ARREST

Suspect Ryan Chen was detained by other responding officers prior to this officer's arrival and was subsequently arrested. A check of records revealed no active warrants for the suspect at this time. This officer notes that the suspect is the named respondent on Protection Order DV-2023-001, which is currently active. Arrest was made for violations including: assault (strangulation), criminal threats (knife), criminal threats (firearm), violation of protection order (presence at victim's residence, contact with victim), and unlawful possession of a firearm in violation of an active protection order. Probable cause for arrest is articulated in the summary above, including the victim's direct testimony, physical evidence of injury, observed scene damage, and confirmed protection order violation. The suspect's emotional state at time of arrest is to be documented in a supplemental report by the arresting officer.

No determination was required as to dual arrest, as no evidence indicated the victim acted as an aggressor or in excess of self-defense.

SECTION 11 — SAFETY PLANNING AND VICTIM SERVICES

The victim was advised of available local domestic violence services and shelter options. The victim expressed interest in referral to local advocacy organizations and requested information about safe housing options. The victim was provided with resource information. The victim expressed a desire to obtain a strengthened protection order and was informed of the process for seeking modification or reinforcement of the existing order. Referral information for children's trauma counseling services was also provided. A threat and risk assessment was conducted with the victim, who described a pattern of escalating violence, prior strangulation incidents, firearm access and threats, stalking behaviors, and an active protection order. Based on this assessment, the threat level is considered HIGH.

SECTION 12 — SPONTANEOUS STATEMENTS

The following spontaneous statements were made by the victim during the interview and are documented verbatim for evidentiary purposes:

- "He tried to strangle me, and I thought I was going to pass out."
- "I thought he was going to kill me. I thought I was going to leave my kids without a mother."
- "You'll never leave me, you'll never take the kids away from me." [This statement was attributed by the victim to the suspect while brandishing the knife.]
- "He said if we didn't do what he wanted, he would get the gun and hurt us."
- "He's been doing this for so long, and I don't know how to make it stop."
- "I'm not going to drop the charges. I'm not going to let him get away with it again."

SECTION 13 — DISPOSITION

Suspect Ryan Chen was placed under arrest and transported to the county detention facility. Victim Leila Jamila Hernandez was transported to the hospital via EMS for evaluation and treatment of injuries. Children were assessed on scene and remained with the victim's support network pending emergency housing arrangements. Evidence (kitchen knife) was collected and submitted to evidence. Scene was photographed and documented. Supplemental reports to be filed by arresting officer and EMS personnel.
